title: Musk Calls on AI Labs to Collaborate on Safety Issues, Wishes to Meld Fences with Altman and Support China's AI in Cooperation
excerpt: During an interview with The Economist, Musk stated that the capabilities of cutting-edge AI models are rapidly improving. He suggested that major AI laboratori
content_html:
During an interview with The Economist, Musk stated that the capabilities of cutting-edge AI models are rapidly improving. He suggested that major AI laboratories should give each other time to review safety issues before releasing new models, and report potential risks separately to the governments in Washington and Beijing. He emphasized that this collaborative mechanism should not only involve American companies, but also Chinese AI developers.

Putting aside personal grievances for the benefit of the world

When asked if he would be willing to collaborate with Sam Altman, CEO of OpenAI, Musk responded, "If we have to talk at the end, we will talk," and added, "For the benefit of the world, I am willing to set aside our personal differences." Musk and Altman are considered two of the biggest rivals in the AI industry. Musk has long accused Altman and OpenAI of straying from the company's original nonprofit mission to pursue commercial interests, leading to multiple lawsuits. Musk has frequently criticized Altman on social media. This public statement of willingness to cooperate marks a rare signal of easing tensions between the two on AI safety issues.

Opposing the ban on Chinese AI, believing China will eventually break through in lithography machines

Musk also explicitly opposed the practice of U.S. companies prohibiting the use of Chinese AI models, arguing that such restrictions would hardly slow down China's AI development. He believes that even if the U.S. continues to restrict China's access to the most advanced technologies, Chinese AI developers will continue to make progress. He also believes that China will eventually solve the problem of insufficient AI chip supply through independent R&D of lithography machines. This statement is notable in the current context of Sino-U.S. technological competition and reflects Musk's consistent advocacy for global collaboration in AI safety governance.

sentences.en: During an interview with The Economist, Musk stated that the capabilities of cutting-edge AI models are rapidly improving.
sentences.zh: 马斯克在接受《经济学人》采访时表示，尖端人工智能模型的能力正在迅速提高。

sentences.en: He suggested that major AI laboratories should give each other time to review safety issues before releasing new models, and report potential risks separately to the governments in Washington and Beijing.
sentences.zh: 他建议各大人工智能实验室在发布新模型之前应该给彼此时间审查安全问题，并分别向华盛顿和北京政府报告潜在风险。

sentences.en: He emphasized that this collaborative mechanism should not only involve American companies, but also Chinese AI developers.
sentences.zh: 他强调，这种协作机制不仅应该包括美国公司，还应该包括中国人工智能开发者。

sentences.en: This public statement of willingness to cooperate marks a rare signal of easing tensions between the two on AI safety issues.
sentences.zh: 这一公开的合作意愿声明标志着两国在人工智能安全问题上紧张关系缓和的罕见信号。

sentences.en: Opposing the ban on Chinese AI, believing China will eventually break through in lithography machines
sentences.zh: 反对禁止中国人工智能，相信中国最终会在光刻机领域取得突破

blocks.html_en: Musk also explicitly opposed the practice of U.S. companies prohibiting the use of Chinese AI models, arguing that such restrictions would hardly slow down China's AI development. He believes that even if the U.S. continues to restrict China's access to the most advanced technologies, Chinese AI developers will continue to make progress. He also believes that China will eventually solve the problem of insufficient AI chip supply through independent R&D of lithography machines. This statement is notable in the current context of Sino-U.S. technological competition and reflects Musk's consistent advocacy for global collaboration in AI safety governance.

sentences.en: Musk also explicitly opposed the practice of U.S. companies prohibiting the use of Chinese AI models, arguing that such restrictions would hardly slow down China's AI development.
sentences.zh: 马斯克还明确反对美国公司禁止使用中国人工智能模型的做法，认为这种限制很难减缓中国人工智能的发展。

sentences.en: He believes that even if the U.S. continues to restrict China's access to the most advanced technologies, Chinese AI developers will continue to make progress.
sentences.zh: 他相信，即使美国继续限制中国获得最先进技术，中国人工智能开发者也将继续取得进步。

sentences.en: He also believes that China will eventually solve the problem of insufficient AI chip supply through independent R&D of lithography machines.
sentences.zh: 他还相信，中国最终将通过光刻机自主研发解决AI芯片供应不足的问题。

sentences.en: This statement is notable in the current context of Sino-U.S. technological competition and reflects Musk's consistent advocacy for global collaboration in AI safety governance.
sentences.zh: 这一表态在当前中美科技竞争的背景下值得注意，也体现了马斯克对人工智能安全治理全球合作的一贯主张。

content_html_zh:
马斯克在接受《经济学人》采访时表示，尖端人工智能模型的能力正在迅速提高。 他建议各大人工智能实验室在发布新模型之前应该给彼此时间审查安全问题，并分别向华盛顿和北京政府报告潜在风险。 他强调，这种协作机制不仅应该包括美国公司，还应该包括中国人工智能开发者。

抛开个人恩怨，造福天下

当被问及是否愿意与 OpenAI 首席执行官萨姆·奥尔特曼合作时，马斯克回答说：“如果我们必须最后谈谈，我们会谈谈，”并补充道，“为了世界的利益，我愿意抛开我们的个人分歧。” 马斯克和奥特曼被认为是人工智能行业最大的两个竞争对手。 马斯克长期以来一直指责 Altman 和 OpenAI 偏离了公司最初的非营利使命，转而追求商业利益，导致多起诉讼。 马斯克经常在社交媒体上批评奥特曼。 这一公开的合作意愿声明标志着两国在人工智能安全问题上紧张关系缓和的罕见信号。

反对禁止中国人工智能，相信中国最终会在光刻机领域取得突破

马斯克还明确反对美国公司禁止使用中国人工智能模型的做法，认为这种限制很难减缓中国人工智能的发展。 他相信，即使美国继续限制中国获得最先进技术，中国人工智能开发者也将继续取得进步。 他还相信，中国最终将通过光刻机自主研发解决AI芯片供应不足的问题。 这一表态在当前中美科技竞争的背景下值得注意，也体现了马斯克对人工智能安全治理全球合作的一贯主张。
